name: angular-best-practices
description: Angular performance optimization and best practices guide. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring Angular code for optimal performance, bundle size, and rendering efficiency.
capabilities: Guide implementation of OnPush change detection and Signals for optimized rendering
Recommend bundle optimization techniques including lazy loading and dynamic imports
Suggest SSR and hydration patterns for improved initial load performance
Identify and eliminate async waterfalls in data fetching patterns
Provide memory management guidelines for subscriptions and cleanup
Review Angular templates for performance anti-patterns
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 107 potential issues, but all are false positives. The skill is pure documentation about Angular best practices. Code block backticks were misidentified as shell execution, dynamic imports are legitimate Angular lazy loading patterns, and URLs are documentation links. No malicious code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Angular applications often suffer from performance issues due to inefficient change detection, bundle bloat, and rendering bottlenecks. This skill provides prioritized best practices to optimize Angular apps for speed and efficiency.
limitations: Does not execute code or make changes to files directly
Cannot measure actual performance metrics without running the application
Focused on Angular-specific patterns, not general JavaScript optimization
Does not provide backend or API optimization guidance

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers building new Angular applications
useCases.title: New Angular Component Development
useCases.description: Use when creating new Angular components to ensure they follow performance best practices from the start, including OnPush strategy and Signals.

useCases.targetUser: Senior developers and tech leads conducting code reviews
useCases.title: Performance Code Review
useCases.description: Use when reviewing existing Angular code to identify performance bottlenecks and receive specific refactoring recommendations.

useCases.title: Angular Upgrade Optimization
useCases.description: Use when migrating Angular applications to newer versions to leverage latest performance features like Zoneless and incremental hydration.

promptTemplates.prompt:

promptTemplates.title: SSR Performance Tuning
promptTemplates.prompt:
Review this Angular setup for server-side rendering performance. Check for:
1. Proper hydration configuration
2. Use of TransferState to prevent duplicate requests
3. Critical vs deferred content separation
4. Any blocking operations in SSR
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing SSR and initial load performance

promptTemplates.title: Complete Performance Audit
promptTemplates.prompt:
Perform a complete performance audit of this Angular code. Categorize findings by priority (Critical, High, Medium, Low) and provide specific recommendations for:
- Change detection strategy
- Data fetching patterns
- Template optimizations
- Memory management
- Bundle structure

bestPractices: Always use ChangeDetectionStrategy.OnPush with Signals for new components - this provides the biggest performance gain
Prefer forkJoin and switchMap over nested subscriptions to eliminate async waterfalls
Use @defer for any component not needed in the initial viewport to reduce initial bundle size
antiPatterns: Avoid default change detection with mutable properties - causes unnecessary re-renders
Avoid nested subscriptions in RxJS - leads to async waterfalls and memory leaks
Avoid eager loading all routes - bloats initial bundle and slows time-to-interactive

faq.question: What is the most impactful Angular performance optimization?
faq.answer: Using OnPush change detection with Signals. This reduces change detection cycles from every event to only when signal values actually change.

faq.question: When should I use lazy loading?
faq.answer: Lazy load all routes that are not required for the initial user experience. Feature modules, heavy charts, and admin panels should all be lazy loaded.

faq.question: What is Zoneless Angular?
faq.answer: Zoneless Angular removes the zone.js dependency, reducing bundle size by ~15KB and eliminating zone.js patches on all async APIs for faster change detection.

faq.question: How do I prevent memory leaks in Angular?
faq.answer: Use takeUntilDestroyed() for RxJS subscriptions, prefer Signals over subscriptions where possible, and clean up any timers or event listeners in ngOnDestroy.

faq.question: What is incremental hydration in Angular?
faq.answer: Incremental hydration allows Angular to hydrate components individually rather than all at once, improving perceived performance especially for larger applications.

faq.question: How does @defer improve performance?
faq.answer: @defer delays loading of component code until specific conditions are met (viewport visibility, user interaction, etc.), reducing initial bundle size and improving time-to-interactive.
